faqs.a: Cognitive flexibility is the brain's ability to adjust. It's what lets a person switch from one task to another, see a problem from a different angle, or change plans when the situation changes. It's one of the core skills psychologists group under executive function.

Yes. It isn't a fixed trait you either have or don't. Like other executive skills it varies from person to person and day to day, and it can be supported through practice, structure, and managing stress.

faqs.q: Why does cognitive flexibility matter for mental health?
faqs.a: Difficulty with it shows up across many conditions, from getting stuck on the same thought in OCD to looping worries in anxiety and depression. Building cognitive flexibility is a quiet goal of many therapies, including cognitive behavioral therapy.
